title: RL for Language Models
concept: PPO is the reinforcement learning algorithm used to update the LLM's weights based on the reward model's scalar scores.

exercises.prompt: Which algorithm is used to update an LLM's weights during the reinforcement learning phase?
exercises.explain: PPO (Proximal Policy Optimization) is the reinforcement learning algorithm used to update the weights of the LLM based on reward feedback.

cards.title: Connecting the Dots
cards.body: We have a Reward Model that can score an LLM's answers. But how does the LLM actually learn and change its behavior from those scores? That is where PPO, the steering wheel of RLHF, comes in!

cards.title: What is PPO?
cards.body: Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) is a Reinforcement Learning algorithm. In LLM training, PPO takes the score (reward) from the reward model and uses it to update the LLM's internal weights. This makes the LLM produce better, more aligned answers next time.

cards.title: The Dog Trainer Analogy
cards.body: Think of training an LLM like teaching a dog to perform tricks. The LLM is the dog, and the Reward Model is the trainer blowing a whistle and throwing treats. PPO is the actual coaching method that helps the dog adjust its muscle movements step-by-step to get more treats without getting confused.

cards.title: PPO in Action
cards.body: When an LLM generates a helpful response like 'Sure, here is how photosynthesis works!', the Reward Model gives it a high score of +9.0. PPO takes this +9.0 score and tweaks the LLM's weights so it repeats this polite and helpful style in the future.

cards.title: Not a New Model!
cards.body: People often think PPO is a brand-new AI model. In reality, PPO is just a formula—an optimization algorithm—that acts as the middleman, updating the LLM's parameters based on the feedback it receives.

cards.title: Scoring vs. Tuning
cards.body: See how the flow of information changes when we introduce PPO to close the training loop.

left.text: Only rates the LLM's outputs, producing a scalar score (e.g., +8.5).

right.text: Uses that score to actively tune and update the LLM's parameters.

exercises.prompt: What role does PPO play in the Reinforcement Learning loop for LLMs?

options.text: It updates the LLM's weights based on the reward scores.
It generates new human prompts automatically.
It translates the text into different languages.

exercises.explain: PPO acts as the bridge that uses the reward model's feedback to adjust the LLM's internal weights.

exercises.prompt: True or False: PPO is used to train and change the weights of the Reward Model itself.
exercises.explain: PPO does not train the reward model; it uses the reward model's scores to update the generator LLM's weights.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing term to complete the sentence explaining the update process.
exercises.sentence: The algorithm used to adjust the LLM's weights based on reward scores is called [blank].
exercises.explain: PPO is the Reinforcement Learning algorithm responsible for taking the reward scores and updating the LLM's parameters.

exercises.prompt: Which of these shows how PPO updates an LLM?

options.text: A human developer manually edits the LLM's code after reading the reward score.
PPO automatically adjusts the LLM's weights using the reward model's scalar scores.
The LLM is completely deleted and retrained from scratch.

exercises.explain: AI-generated training loops use PPO to systematically update LLM weights based on scores. Manual prompt editing is done by humans.

exercises.prompt: As the ultimate test, can you summarize how PPO uses the reward model to improve the LLM?

options.text: PPO uses the reward model's scalar scores to update the LLM's weights.
PPO completely rewrites the reward model from scratch.
PPO bypasses the reward model and lets the LLM teach itself.
PPO changes the prompt instead of updating any model weights.

exercises.explain: PPO uses the scalar rewards generated by the reward model (which rates the text quality) to systematically adjust the LLM's weights, making it more likely to produce high-scoring answers.
